_comment: PER-BOT config — install at ~/.cc-channel-octo/<id>/config.json (the <id> must match an entry in the global config's `bots` list; for a single bot use id 'default'). This file is the highest-priority layer: it overrides both the inline bots[] entry and the global shared fields. It holds the bot's REQUIRED botToken. The bot's directories are derived automatically as siblings of this file: ./data, ./workspace, ./memory. Drop a ./SOUL.md next to this file to give the bot a personality (it overrides sdk.systemPrompt).
_comment_overrides: Any of these optional fields override the global config for THIS bot only:
_comment_apiUrl: apiUrl — override the Octo API base for this bot.

sdk._comment_model: Override the model for this bot, e.g. 'vertexai/claude-opus-4-8'.
sdk._comment_systemPrompt: Inline personality string. A ./SOUL.md file next to this config overrides it.
sdk._comment_skills: Per-bot skill selection: 'all' or a string[] of skill names from the shared library (~/.cc-channel-octo/skills + this bot's ./skills).
sdk._comment_env: Extra env injected into the agent's tool subprocess, e.g. {"OCTO_BOT_ID":"<robotId>"} so a shared CLI acts as this bot.
sdk._comment_cron: Set cron=true to let the agent schedule tasks (cron_create/list/delete → ./cron.json, owner-gated).
sdk._comment_mcp_servers: Per-bot external MCP servers (mcp__<name>__<tool>), same shape as the global sdk.mcpServers. A per-bot map fully REPLACES the global one (sdk blocks merge shallowly), so list every server this bot needs. http header values are secrets — keep this file non-world-readable (chmod 600). Omit to inherit the global map.
